title: OpenAPI 3.2, Compatibility, Deployment, Draining, and Recovery · Stakeholder TypeScript Mission
summary: An engineering, security, accessibility, data, client, and operations review board. The board needs layered tests, fault and load evidence, bounded telemetry and health, profiles and capacity, an accurate OpenAPI 3.2 contract, compatibility policy, safe migrations, canary and drain proof, rollback, data recovery, and a defended runbook. Planned artifact: Deliver Production TypeScript API and Recovery Defense with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extensio…
objectives: Inject aborts, timeouts, pool exhaustion, ambiguous commits, proxy faults, slow streams, clock shifts, load, and resource leaks with invariant and recovery gates.
Connect service-level indicators and objectives to multi-window alerts, deploy and trace evidence, bounded incident diagnosis, mitigation, recovery, and learning.
Gate changed builds with noise-aware baselines, representative cases, resource ceilings, overload recovery, rollback thresholds, and retained profiles.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Project Fault Time Load Leak Gates
steps.instruction:
project case — A project team handles case 8946 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate one contract-compatible, expand-first, canary, drain, and recoverable release; preserve response ownership after a client abort; then change the method and preserve both status and durable-effect evidence. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Inject aborts, timeouts, pool exhaustion, ambiguous commits, proxy faults, slow streams, clock shifts, load, and resource leaks with invariant and recovery gates.

Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate one contract-compatible, expand-first, canary, drain, and recoverable release; preserve response ownership after a client abort; then fail the canary while the old revision drains and prove rollback remains safe. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
